summary: Backend-only end-to-end testing of Taxi App. Validated OTP dev fallback auth, profile completion, fare estimation, full ride lifecycle (create -> driver online -> requests -> accept -> arrived -> in_transit -> completed), driver earnings/ride count increment, rider rating updating driver avg, /rides/active, /rides/my, ownership 403 enforcement, role-based 403 (driver cannot book), and Stripe Checkout session creation. 9/9 pytest tests pass.

critical.issue: Returns 500 (uncaught CheckoutError: 'No such checkout.session') when polling status with the placeholder STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent. The created session_id is not retrievable via Stripe API and the exception is not caught. Wrap stripe.get_checkout_status in try/except and return a structured 4xx (or graceful pending) so the rider polling page does not crash.

minor.issue: Status transitions are not validated (e.g., can jump from 'searching' to 'completed' if driver_id is set, and cancelled-after-completed check uses tuple ('completed',) but other transitions are unchecked). Consider an explicit allowed-transition map.
No idempotency / re-rate guard. A rider can rate the same completed ride multiple times, each call re-aggregates the driver rating.
Allows overwriting role on every call (rider can flip to driver or vice-versa) and does not require driver vehicle fields when role='driver' (vehicle_make/model/plate are Optional).
_dev_otps is in-process memory; multi-worker deploys would break OTP verify. OK for single-worker dev only.

action_items: Wrap emergentintegrations get_checkout_status() in try/except in /api/payments/status/{session_id}; return 200 with payment_status='pending' or 502 with message instead of 500. This is the only failing flow.
Add ride status-transition validation (searching->accepted->arrived->in_transit->completed; cancellable until in_transit).
Make rate endpoint idempotent (reject if rating already set, or update without re-aggregating unnecessarily).
Validate driver vehicle_make/model/plate are required when role='driver' in complete-profile; consider preventing role change after first set.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 600+ lines — split into routers/auth.py, routers/rides.py, routers/driver.py, routers/payments.py, models.py, deps.py for maintainability.
create_checkout reads request body manually with request.json(); should use a Pydantic body model (CheckoutReq with origin_url) for validation and OpenAPI docs.
Stripe success_url contains literal '{CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}' template — confirm emergentintegrations expands it; otherwise rider is redirected with the literal string.
JWT_SECRET default is hardcoded in .env ('change-in-prod-2026') — fine for dev, but flag for prod rotation.
_dev_otps in-memory dict won't scale across uvicorn workers — acceptable since Twilio fallback path is intended only for dev.

success_rate.backend: 100% (9/9 functional tests pass) — but /payments/status returns 500 in current Stripe sandbox; counted as known issue, not a test failure

test_credentials: OTP dev fallback. Phones used during run: rider +1415555<5digit-epoch>, driver +1415666<5digit-epoch>. dev_code returned in send-otp response.
seed_data_creation: Created 3 users (rider, driver, secondary rider) and ~3 rides per run. Phones suffixed with epoch to avoid collision. No cleanup performed; recommend wiping taxi_app DB if accumulation matters.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py as regression suite. Re-run after the /payments/status 500 fix. Phones are auto-uniquified per epoch run; if running multiple times in same second, change SUFFIX. Frontend has not been tested — only backend per request.
rca of the issue: GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} -> 500. Reproduction: POST /api/payments/checkout/{ride_id} succeeds and returns session_id starting with cs_test_... Then GET /api/payments/status/{that_id} triggers emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status which calls Stripe API, gets back 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' and raises CheckoutError. The route handler does not catch it, so FastAPI returns 500. Mitigation: try/except CheckoutError -> return {'payment_status': 'pending', 'status': 'open'} or HTTPException(502, 'Payment provider unavailable').
